📅 Daily Events

🗓 Monthly Events

Month-scale events don't hold the day clock — anything still pending when the month is committed gets a forced decision (accept / reject / skip) in the 🏰 Monthly Turn tab.

📊 World Import / Export — bulk Domains · Hexes · Settlements · Lairs as one .xlsx workbook
Reading workbook…

⬡ Hexes

Hexes live in the top-level campaign.hexes[] collection (mirrored per-domain under domain.geography.hexes[] for owned hexes). This view aggregates across all domains and unclaimed wilderness — use “+ add hex → Unclaimed wilderness” to create a domainless hex, or a hex's Domain field to claim/release it. Settlement/economy fields stay synced with the Domain > Hexes tab.

🗣 Rumors

+ at:

Spread copies a rumor to every other settlement in the same domain, downgrading apparent level one tier (common → uncommon → rare → obscure). Already-reached settlements are skipped, so the action is idempotent. Use this to model proliferation manually when you want finer control than the per-turn drift tick.

📍 Points of Interest

🕳️ Dungeons

First-class delve targets in campaign.dungeons[] (Phase 3.5). Lifecycle overlays Attuned / Owned onto the stored status (derived — dungeonLifecycleLabel). Encounters left is the authored count, or — for a dungeon stocked with lairs (lair.dungeonId) — the living-lair count. The abstract-foray resolver + the Foray Wizard land in D3.

New domain (RR pp.353–354)

Domain type

A clanhold can later be decreed Transitional, then converted to Ordinary (RR p.354) — Transitional is reached through play, not chosen at creation.

A barbarian clanhold cannot host a senate (RR p.354).

Marks the realm senatorial now; generate its senators & factions later from the Governance ▸ Senate tab.

📤 Export for player

Download a redacted, player-safe .acks.json — only this player's characters & domains plus the discovered world. Hand it out instead of the GM save; secrets and unexplored hexes stay yours.

🏗 Build a dungeon (RR p.386)

Mints a first-class Dungeon — the construction completes now (the RAW-timed Construction Wizard lands later). Monsters then auto-arrive + lair within it via Vagaries of Incursion (when that rule is on). An arcane L9+ owner is auto-attuned.

🏚 Lairs

First-class placed lairs in campaign.lairs[] (RAW core, #476). Unauthored = a seeded shell awaiting contents (populate it from the lair's detail panel); dynamic pool = authored but unplaced (JJ p.195), revealed into a hex on demand. 🙈 = the players haven't discovered it.

🪙 Stashes

Located, owned inventories in campaign.stashes[]. A domain's treasury is a stash that transfers with the office, not the ruler. Total sums all coin (gp-equivalent across cp/sp/ep/gp/pp) + valuables; weight derives per RR p.83 (1,000 coins = 1 stone).

⚔ Encounters

The RAW pre-combat procedure (RR pp.280–287) walks each encounter in its resolution panel: distance → surprise → evasion → reaction → parley → outcome. Past encounters persist as the world's memory — meet the same lair or band again and the panel recalls the last attitude shown above.

🎌 Battles

The RR battle engine (pp.461–472): ~10-minute battle turns of missile and melee per zone, morale collapse at the break point, then pursuit, casualties, spoils, and XP. Heroes join as heroic units or zoom in through heroic forays — the foray fight itself is yours to run at the table.

⚗ New research project

Research (labor over time):
Components (paid at the throw):

🎖 Add to which army?

🚶 Muster a unit

Construction is labor-paid and takes time (RR p.174). This raises an under-construction project — advance the Day Clock / Advance Month and it builds toward completion, then becomes a real structure. (The instant + add component on the Stronghold tab is the admin shortcut.)

Owner:

Workforce — RR p.174 (laborers 5 cf/day, craftsmen 25)

Supervisor(s) — engineer ≤100,000gp / siege engineer ≤25,000gp (RR p.174)

🎺 Followers arrive · RR p.334

🏚 Lair Wizard

Author one lair by hand (catalog-free). Inhabitants is a manual estimate for now — a later milestone generates structured groups + treasure from the monster catalog.

Destination

Held unplaced (no hex) — reveal it into a hex later. The encounter pipeline can draw a pooled dynamic lair on a lair roll (a later milestone).

Roll the RAW wilderness lair density for an unsettled hex (JJ p.69, Lairs per Hex) — it creates that many empty, undetailed lair shells you flesh out later. Hexes inside a domain seed none (securing a hex clears its lairs).

🎖 Muster an Army · RR pp.435–437

A commander raises a field force from the troops he controls. Station units now or add them later; reorganize divisions on the Armies view.

Station units

Units at the muster point fall in now; troops elsewhere are called up — they march to the muster point (no teleporting).

troops present · BR

🛒 Trade —

Buy or sell at a market — gated by what it stocks (RR p.124); paid through the purse.

Step 1Who, where, which way
Market
🎖 · Class
🪙 Purse: Market: 🛒 Campaign market: — the baggage train (RR p.452)
Total: Load: Activity:

🌿 Forage / 🏹 Hunt —

Live off the land (RR p.278). Forage = one ancillary task; hunting takes the day (dedicated).

Search the hex —

One search-hour = one ancillary task; each hour also risks an encounter (RR pp.276–277). The Judge rolls secretly — a failure tells the players nothing.

🐾 Track them

Find the trail (Tracking 11+, RR p.120 — one turn's search; no retry for an hour on a miss), then FOLLOW: the party's journey is steered after the band at half expedition speed, day by day, until caught or the trail is lost (it enters water, or an hour of rain/snow falls). Following itself needs no throw.

🗺 Campaign Map

Scroll to zoom · drag to pan · click a hex to open its card

➕ Create Map

Lay out a rectangular grid of blank hexes — no terrain, unexplored, unclaimed — a clean canvas to build a world on. Hexes you already have are kept (never overwritten); the grid fills in around them.

📖 Annals · the chronicle of the whole realm

Every recorded event, newest first — filter by the kind of thing that happened.

📥 Event Log · pending · in log

🔧 The Event Log is the structured audit trail — every typed event with its full payload, submitter, target turn, and result. Pending events can be resolved here (accept / reject / inline edit) without waiting for the month commit; the day-scale effects queue under and the domain-turn events under .

📋 Activities

Activities under way today — each can be rejected to roll it back (untick a domain administration, refund a market trade, or open a journey to stop it). A character's full day-activity budget and the actions they can take now live in Roster ▸ Characters — select a character there.

Mercantile Ventures

Active ventures

Venture history

Venturers

Named venturers (PCs or NPCs) who run ventures across the campaign. Stats auto-update from venture history.

Passive Investment

Per ACKS II RR p.383: monthly return = capital × risk-tier rate. Three RAW investment types: commercial expedition (mercantile ventures someone else runs, Balanced/Risky only), business establishment (inn, store, smithy, guild — all 5 tiers), money lending (loans, all 5 tiers). A commercial-expedition investment owned by a venturer competes with that venturer's active arbitrage for capital — the UI prompts to pause one when the other starts. Vagaries-on-passive-income and graceful liquidation queued as task #97.

📖 Merchandise reference · ACKS II RR p.374

29 merchandise types available in markets across the Auran Empire. Daily figures are stones available at market impact 1; multiply by market impact for the actual quantity. Tariff per RR p.372 — 5% on common, 0% on grain & vegetables, 20% on precious.

Merchandise Container Base price / st Price step Tariff Class I Class II Class III Class IV Class V Class VI

Generic averages (for quick estimates per RR's Designer's Note): common 1.1gp/st (0.025 st/urban-family/day) · precious 135gp/st (0.00074 st/urban-family/day) · any-merchandise 3.1gp/st (0.0168 st/urban-family/day).

🌊 Vagaries of Investment · distilled from RR pp.383-388

Per in-transit venture per turn, one entry is rolled and surfaced in the staged monthly turn (🏰 Monthly Turn ▸ Investments). Effects are applied at commit (GM can override or reroll). Weights total 90 + 10 calm-voyage buffer = 100 — distribution roughly 50% no-event, 22% minor setbacks, 15% good outcomes, 12% bad/catastrophic.

VagaryWeightSeverityEffectDescription

🗡 Hijinks

Downtime crime & intrigue (RR pp.360–370). A perpetrator plans, performs, and lays low over a month — the Day Clock advances it.

Inspector · Browse, inspect, edit any entity in your campaign

✨ Create new

⚙ House Rules

Tick a rule to enable it for this campaign. Enabling a rule may unlock new options or panels elsewhere in the tool. State is saved with the campaign file. Rules grouped by topic — pick a sub-tab to focus.

Each rule is a self-contained toggle with a RAW citation in its description. Most rules are off by default — turn on what fits your campaign style.

🧭 Parties

🎖 Armies

Select a party, army, or unit above to see its details and actions.

🏘 Settlements

🏚 Lairs

First-class placed lairs in campaign.lairs[] (RAW core, #476). Unauthored = a seeded shell awaiting contents (populate it from the lair's detail panel); dynamic pool = authored but unplaced (JJ p.195), revealed into a hex on demand. 🙈 = the players haven't discovered it.

🐾 Groups

migrant + pursuing + tracked + homeward bands — monsters not settled in a lair

A derived view — migrants are living campaign.groups[] whose lair is gone (or who never had one); pursuers are active chases (⚙ Monster pursuit) and tracked bands are live follows (🐾, RR p.120), each read from its encounter; 🏠 homeward bands are hunters walking back to their den after a chase; 🏴 banditry bands are a low-morale domain's own men (RR pp.350–351 — ⚙ Domain banditry; raised and disbanded by the monthly turn, raiding within their domain). Migrants wander with the Day Clock (half speed, random, never doubling straight back — ⚙ Persistent wandering monsters); one that wanders into a domain rolls its JJ p.103 disposition and may settle as a lair above.

⛵ Ships

🤝 Recruit Hirelings · RR pp.164–167

Solicit · Engage · Negotiate · Hire

New passive investment

Per RR p.383: monthly return = capital × tier rate. The choice of type determines which risk tiers are allowed (commercial expedition uses Balanced/Risky only; business establishments and money lending can use all five).

Buy price defaults to basePrice + (priceStep × originDM). Click ↻ to recompute after changing origin or DM. Override manually for negotiated rates or unusual circumstances.

🛒 Settle venture

⛪ Religion & Divine Power

A divine caster gathers the faithful, accrues divine power (a gp-equivalent resource that fades one month after it's received — RR p.422), and spends it to bless his realm or returns it to his god for experience. Congregations generate power each monthly turn (Advance Month); a ruler/chaplain who leads his subjects in worship draws power scaled by domain morale. Core RAW — no house rule gates it.

🛐 Deities

⛪ Congregations

Weekly DP = 10gp per 50 personal congregants (full rate) + (families ÷ 10) × the morale rate for domain worship (RR p.421). Monthly DP accrues to the high priest on Advance Month.

📿 Divine power

Divine power is spent on consecration or returned for XP. It cannot be hoarded — each accrual fades one month after it's received (RR p.422). Pray-and-sacrifice grants campaign XP for the month's returned power above your XP threshold, settled at Advance Month.

👑 Ascended Incarnations

The apotheosis ritual (Magic Research) transfigures a creature into a deathless incarnation — a divine being. Thereafter it must consume divine power = 6% of its XP value every day or lose Hit Dice; at 0 HD it sinks into a deathless torpor until worship resumes. It may also channel divine power into vitality (1 campaign XP per gp). The daily upkeep settles on the Day Clock (and at Advance Month). Core RAW — no house rule.

A Death save: success → an incarnation; a natural 1 (or a failed save) → obliterated forever.

🩸 Blood Sacrifice · the Chaotic path · RR pp.421–422

A divine caster spills a victim's life to earn divine power. The base (= the victim's component value, usually its XP value) is always earned; the victim-characteristic multipliers are added only on a successful magic-research throw. Lawful/Neutral casters can only sacrifice helpless animals — never sapient creatures (RR p.422); a Chaotic caster takes a willing victim. Warlock/demonologist Power of Sacrifice casters store arcane power ×2 instead.

⏳ Advance the Day Clock to accrue divine power at its true weekly grain (one week each on days 7 / 14 / 21 / 28 of the month); the day-tick review lists each week's accrual. Otherwise it batches on Advance Month — the two cadences total the same power for the month (RR p.422; Plan §5.7).

🏯 Sieges

An army invests a garrisoned stronghold or urban settlement. By default the siege resolves simplified — the Duration-of-Siege table (shp × the besieger's unit advantage → days to capture). Switch to detailed for blockade, bombardment, and assault (RR pp.473–485).

🏦 Banking & Loans

Commercial & personal credit and deposit custody (RR p.42 Access to Capital + RR p.313). Interest accrues each monthly turn (Advance Month): 3% / month uncollateralized, 1% collateralized; unpaid interest capitalizes onto the balance and the borrower becomes disreputable (RR p.42). A market lends up to its class's monthly capital pool. Core RAW — no house rule gates it.

Loans

🏦 Bank Accounts

Letters of Credit

Draw a note against a bank account (the 📜 Issue letter button on an account above), drawable at a different market — the gold never travels (RR p.42). Redeeming pays the bearer at the destination market, capped by that market's monthly capital pool. A reasoned tooling extension.

🪄 Magic Items

|
NameKindRarityBase costChargesPage

The magic-item economy (#143) over campaign.notableItems[]: identify (5 RAW methods, rarity-gated), appraise (the TT p.28 price spread), and use charges. Buying/selling routes through the existing market verbs; item creation is Magic Research's. The catalog ships mechanical fields + a page-ref only — the effect text stays in your book.

📚 Knowledge · the Lore your campaign tracks — who knows what, how reliably

＋ Record a fact

A fact in the world (the true statement). Who holds it, and how reliably, is tracked separately below.

🗣 Promote a rumor → Lore

A campaign rumor becomes a tracked rumor-kind fact here — who believes it, and how reliably, is then tracked like any Lore. The rumor stays in its settlement panel.

Lore

What a character knows

🧙 NPC Generator

Roll a complete RAW NPC (JJ Ch.9) — occupation → attributes → class/level → HD·AC·saves → proficiencies → age → wealth → appearance. The generated NPC lands in your 👥 Characters roster.

🛠 Commission a magic item

A patron pays an NPC caster to craft an item (TT p.28: 3× base = material + components up front, the fee on success). It routes into a Magic Research project — the patron pays, the caster researches. It can fail.

⚔ Gladiators

Run a gladiatorial school (AXIOMS 4): recruit gladiators, schedule + resolve arena bouts, and stage games. Bouts resolve abstractly (1d10, RR p.25); casualties run through the Mortal-Wounds resolver and winners earn XP equal to the defeated's value.

Generate a senate · RR pp.355–360

Convene a senate over a realm's apex domain. Its size derives from the realm's families; its leading senators are drawn from the realm's actual notables (vassal lords, magistrates, court, residents), each with rolled influence, 1d3 policy objectives, and a faction. Re-roll, seat extra notables, then materialize it.

✎ Edit · RR pp.355–360

Each vote is a named senator. A leading senator's bloc is the patron plus their clients; the rest are independent minor senators. Drag a client or independent between leading senators (or to/from the independent pool) to re-align them; the vote tallies follow.

🏙 Settlement Visits — off-screen town stays (JJ ch.3)

＋ Start a stay
Who's in town:

A holed-up stay makes one urban-incident check per day on the Day Clock (5+ on 1d6, JJ p.80). The daily check is seeded — re-opening the Day-Tick review shows the same incident. A combat-risk incident can be ⚔ escalated to a full Encounter. Wandering / looking-for-trouble are GM-pressed — use 🎲 Roll an incident.

Visits

🛒 Magic-item market

Buy / sell / appraise at a settlement market. Price is the TT p.28 spread (buy ×2.25, sell-created ×2, sell-found ×1); a bigger market handles rarer goods. The coin move rides GP Wave B; the item is a notableItem.

Governance —

⚔ Change in management

👥 Roster & Entourage

Generate a batch of NPCs — a settlement's notables, a ruler's court, or a wandering party — then review (re-roll or drop a row) and add the keepers to your 👥 Characters roster.
